delta.comment: Corrected Ship workflow gap after execution: created and switched to `feat/auto-tune-follow-up-hardening` from `main` using `git checkout -b feat/auto-tune-follow-up-hardening`. The existing dirty worktree was preserved; no files were discarded.

Prepared 005-S for the next Ship step on branch `feat/auto-tune-follow-up-hardening`. Ran CLI smoke gate with `uv run autoharness --help` successfully. Staged review scope is limited to `.github/agents/auto-tune.agent.md`, `.github/skills/tune-harness/SKILL.md`, `src/autoharness/verify_workspace.py`, and `tests/test_verify_workspace.py`. Remaining `.backlogit/`, `.autoharness/`, local agent migrations, workspace settings, and session-memory artifacts remain unstaged as dirty-worktree noise or separate operational artifacts.

User manually committed and pushed the shipment work on branch `feat/auto-tune-follow-up-hardening` to `origin/feat/auto-tune-follow-up-hardening`. Traceability commit: `7e6c61344df283d72f8c655b160f2494d8192b9a` (`feat: Enhance autoharness with new Ship and Stage agents, improving backlog management and execution pipelines`). Shipment is now ready for the review / PR phase.

Post-push review/cleanup plan: 1) fix the blocking verifier behavior so `learning_signals` does not depend on `docs/compound` existing before closure/continuous-learning sources can contribute; 2) decide whether committed self-install outputs (`.autoharness/**`, operator-local config, generated queue/log state) belong on this branch, because current manifest null checksums make repo-level verify-workspace noisy; 3) if retaining the backlog bookkeeping changes, make a dedicated follow-up commit for the current `.backlogit` residue so the branch is clean again before PR preparation.
